title: Aneilema nudicaule
taxonomy: Aneilema nudicaule is classified within the family Commelinaceae, commonly known as the spiderwort family. This family comprises around 600 species of flowering plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Aneilema nudicaule is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Aneilema, which consists of around 80 species. The genus Aneilema is native to tropical Africa and Madagascar. Aneilema nudicaule is further classified as a perennial herb with small white flowers.
uses: Aneilema nudicaule has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. Its roots and leaves are valued for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have wound-healing properties and is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Additionally, it is used to treat gastrointestinal problems and fever. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Aneilema nudicaule.

side_effects: Aneilema nudicaule, a plant species, has been reported to cause skin irritation in some individuals due to its milky latex.
